title: InSAR-prioritised inspection on a high-speed corridor
summary: EGMS ground-motion data helped prioritise maintenance inspection on a 50 km high-speed section, catching subsidence before track defects appeared.
challenge: Embankments along a 50 km section showed early signs of differential settlement. Visual patrols and sparse ground sensors were too slow to characterise movement extent or prioritise intervention across the full corridor.
approach: EGMS displacement products derived from Sentinel-1 provided line-of-sight velocities at regular grid spacing. Analysis of multi-year velocity maps revealed three subsidence hotspots correlated with clay geology and drainage features.
results: Geotechnical teams were deployed only to the prioritised locations, cutting unnecessary inspection mobilisations sharply. One site was escalated to remediation before any visible defect appeared, avoiding a potential speed restriction affecting well over a hundred trains per day.
